rulings.Case Title: FLICK, INC. VS. MING CHAO LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Respondent Ming Chao Liu) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Arbitration Award And Request For Order Restraining Flick Inc. From Asserting Unsupported Fraud Claims
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2025, Line 1. RESPONDENT MING LIU's Motion To Seal Arbitration Award And Request For Order Restraining Flick Inc. From Asserting Unsupported Fraud Claims. Respondent Ming Chao Liu's motion to seal arbitration award and request for restraining order is denied. Records of litigation are presumptively public; the court must make specific factual findings to seal them. (See Rule of Court 2.550(d).) Respondent provides no factual support on which the court could make factual findings to seal the record. Moreover, in this case, the arbitration award that was confirmed by the court is the basis for a judgment against Respondent. It is hard to imagine what adequate basis would underlie sealing an entire arbitration award that forms the basis for a public judgment. The request to seal the arbitration award is denied. Respondent also seeks a restraining order to prevent Petitioner Flick Inc. "from publicly asserting or presenting fraud allegations not supported by legal evidence." Respondent provides no legal authority and this court has no freestanding power to enjoin speech or prevent parties from making litigation claims. The court denies this request as well. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ELISE WILLIAMS ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant City And County Of San Francisco To Produce Its Person(S) Most Qualified For Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFFS ELISE WILLIAMS, AND ZACHARY WILLIAMS' Motion To Compel Defendant City And County Of San Francisco To Produce Its Person(S) Most Qualified For Deposition. Plaintiffs Elise Williams and Zachary Williams' motion to compel defendant the City and County of San Francisco to produce a PMQ for deposition is granted. Defendant Leitmotif Services LLC is in bankruptcy proceedings. The Williamses seek to depose the City's PMQ despite the bankruptcy stay. The bankruptcy stay applies only to proceedings against Leitmotif and not to proceedings against the City. (See Higgins v. Superior Court (2017) 15 Cal.App.5th 973, 981 ["Although [the bankruptcy] stay was automatic as a matter of law when [co-defendant] Powell filed his chapter 7 petition . . ., that stay applied only to the debtor, defendant Powell, not to the nondebtor, codefendant Higgins.") The City does not really argue to the contrary but rather expresses the concern that when the stay is lifted, Leitmotif may seek to take another PMQ deposition that duplicates the deposition that the Williamses seek. This hypothetical concern is insufficient reason to informally stay discovery now where trial is set for October, a date not far away. The court therefore grants the motion and orders the parties to set a mutually convenient deposition date within 30 days of this hearing. No party seeks sanctions, and in any event the court finds both parties' positions to have substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. WILD PLANET FOODS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2025, Line 6. DEFENDANT WILD PLANET FOODS, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses. Defendant Wild Planet Foods, Inc. moves to compel Gabriel Espinoza's further responses to its special interrogatories and requests for production, and seeks sanctions. The initial responses were deficient, but before filing an opposition, Espinoza served amended responses that provided significantly more information than his initial responses. Wild Planet contends the amended responses remain deficient. Wild Planet's motion was likewise deficient in that it did not provide 16 days' notice, although the parties agreed to the hearing date. The court has discretion to decide a motion to compel, and to award sanctions, notwithstanding that supplemental responses were served while the motion was pending. (See Cal. Rules of Court 3.1030(a).) The court also has discretion to excuse an untimely notice of motion, except in specific contexts like summary judgment. But on the facts presented here, where Espinoza has served substantive responses, the court concludes that it would be better in the first instance to permit the parties to meet and confer and try to narrow any remaining disputes. The court continues the matter for hearing to July 22, 2025 concerning any further responses ordered and any sanctions. The parties are ordered to meet and confer telephonically or in person. Wild Planet may file a supplemental brief of no more than five pages explaining what relief it continues to seek and why no later than two weeks in advance of the continued hearing date. Espinoza may respond in a supplemental brief of no more than five pages no later than one week in advance of the continued hearing date. Wild Planet may take its motion off calendar if the parties resolve their discovery dispute by providing three days' notice to the court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.
